profile.name: Izzat Zakhir
profile.role: I am a third year student at Universiti Putra Malaysia and pursuing a degree in Computer and Communication Systems Engineering
profile.intro: I have big passion in esports and sports especially involving analytics. Because of that, I have been building projects that are focused on sports and esports analytics. I am always looking for ways to turn complex stats into interfaces that feel approachable and easy to scan.
profile.availability: Open to internships, freelance work, and collaborative side projects

hero.title: Building sports products that make complex stats feel simple.

stats.label: Availability
stats.value: Open for 10 weeks internship relating to Computer and Communication Systems Engineering field

values.title: Stats that stay readable
values.text: I like turning large datasets into interfaces that feel easy to scan instead of intimidating.

values.title: Built around real fans
values.text: The best sports products respect urgency, curiosity, and the way people naturally jump between overview and deep dive.

values.title: Flexible by design
values.text: I enjoy building products that can support both casual browsing and power-user exploration without feeling messy.

projectsSection.label: Projects
projectsSection.title: Two projects, one clear direction.
projectsSection.copy: These builds show the kind of work I want to keep pushing forward: sports products with strong information design, clear interaction patterns, and interfaces that help users move naturally between overview and analysis.

projects.title: MPL MY Statistics Dashboard
projects.summary: A multi-season MPL MY website built to help fans explore schedules, standings, rosters, hero stats, and matchup context without getting buried in raw tables.
projects.impact: Takes league data from two seasons and turns it into a cleaner experience with season-aware routing, deeper player views, and comparison tools.
highlights: Supports MPL MY Season 16 and Season 17 with direct-link season routing
Includes schedules, team standings, player stats, heroes, hero pool, player pool, and head-to-head tools
Uses popups and filters to keep a large stats surface browsable on desktop and mobile

projects.title: F1 Dual Analytics
projects.summary: A Formula 1 analytics platform with two experiences in one product: Casual Mode for fast race-weekend browsing and Nerd Mode for engineering-style breakdowns.
projects.impact: Combines a React frontend with a FastAPI backend so users can move from standings and results into telemetry, tyre strategy, lap comparison, and simulation tools.
highlights: Covers seasons from 2021 to 2026 with round-level navigation and responsive layouts
Ships telemetry, tyre strategy, H2H, race engineer, and prediction-focused views for deeper analysis
Blends fan-friendly browsing with data-heavy workflows instead of forcing a single interface style

about.label: About
about.title: I like shaping data into something people can actually use.
about.copy: A lot of my current work sits inside sports and esports because those spaces demand quick scanning, strong hierarchy, and interfaces that still hold up when the data gets dense.
points: MPL MY Statistics Dashboard shows how I handle broad feature surfaces with filters, modals, and multi-season content.
F1 Dual Analytics shows that I can also think in systems, separating casual browsing from deeper technical analysis in the same product.
Across both projects, the common thread is making information-rich experiences feel fast, intentional, and approachable.

skillsSection.label: Skills
skillsSection.title: What I bring to a project.
skillsSection.copy: My edge is strongest when interface design, frontend implementation, and information-heavy content need to work together instead of being treated separately.

strengths: React, TypeScript, and modern web workflows

contact.label: Contact
contact.title: Let's build products that are clear, fast, and worth exploring.
contact.copy: Open to internships, freelance work, and collaborative side projects. You can reach me directly by email, connect with me on LinkedIn, or explore more of my work on GitHub.
